completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/fleet/manifest.py defining FORGE_MANIFEST: AgentManifest = AgentManifest(...) at module level and listing it in __all__. TestForgeManifestExport::test_module_exports_constant asserts hasattr(manifest_module, 'FORGE_MANIFEST').

manifest.py imports AgentManifest, IntentCapability, ToolCapability directly from nats_core.manifest with no local subclass or rebinding. TestForgeManifestType asserts isinstance(FORGE_MANIFEST, AgentManifest) AND type(FORGE_MANIFEST) is AgentManifest (the class object identity must match the one exported by nats_core).

FORGE_MANIFEST sets agent_id='forge', trust_tier='core', max_concurrent=1 verbatim. TestForgeManifestIdentity has one test per field plus a supplemental test asserting name='Forge', template='deepagents-pipeline-orchestrator', version='0.1.0', status='ready'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Three `IntentCapability` entries (build.* / pipeline.* / feature.*) match §2.1 verbatim
completion_promises.evidence: manifest.py declares exactly three IntentCapability entries with patterns build.* (signals=['build','develop','implement','create','make','ship'], confidence=0.90), pipeline.* (signals=['pipeline','stages','progress','status','deploy'], confidence=0.85), and feature.* (signals=['feature','add feature','new capability','requirement'], confidence=0.80) — each with the descriptions copied verbatim from §2.1. TestForgeManifestIntents has one test per intent verifying pattern, signals list, confidence (pytest.approx), and description string.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Five `ToolCapability` entries (forge_greenfield, forge_feature, forge_review_fix, forge_status, forge_cancel) match §2.1 verbatim
completion_promises.evidence: manifest.py declares exactly five ToolCapability entries with names forge_greenfield (mutating, async, requires_approval=False, parameters require repo+feature_yaml_path with branch default 'main'), forge_feature (mutating, async, repo+feature_id), forge_review_fix (mutating, async, repo+subject), forge_status (read_only, optional pipeline_id), forge_cancel (mutating, pipeline_id required, optional reason). All returns strings copied verbatim. TestForgeManifestTools has test_five_tools, test_tool_names (set comparison), and one test per tool comparing parameters dict, returns string, risk_level, async_mode, requires_approval, and description.

FORGE_MANIFEST.required_permissions is set to ['graphiti:read','graphiti:write','filesystem:read','filesystem:write','shell:execute','nats:publish','nats:subscribe','network:github.com'] in declaration order matching §2.1. TestForgeManifestPermissions::test_required_permissions asserts equality with the full list (order-sensitive).

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] **Secret-free**: `FORGE_MANIFEST.model_dump_json()` contains none of `"api_key"`, `"token"`, `"password"`, `"secret"`, `"credential"` (case-insensitive) — asserted by unit test
completion_promises.evidence: TestForgeManifestSecretFree::test_no_secret_substrings is parametrised over the five forbidden needles ['api_key','token','password','secret','credential'] and asserts re.search(needle, FORGE_MANIFEST.model_dump_json(), flags=re.IGNORECASE) is None. All five parametrised cases pass against the current manifest.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Import path `from forge.fleet.manifest import FORGE_MANIFEST` resolves
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/fleet/__init__.py and src/forge/fleet/manifest.py exist; package is discoverable via the existing setuptools src layout (where=['src']). TestForgeManifestExport::test_import_path_resolves directly imports `from forge.fleet.manifest import FORGE_MANIFEST` and a supplemental test_package_reexport imports from the package root. Both pass under pytest.
